channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18955] sanityn test_15: Timeout occurred
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/eae3c607-8193-4940-8472-f35d35dd3637

test_15 failed with the following error:

Timeout occurred after 268 minutes, last suite running was sanityn

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/112808 - 4.18.0-553.46.1.el8_10.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/112808 - 4.18.0-553.46.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanityn test_15 - Timeout occurred after 268 minutes, last suite running was sanityn

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment:
17 records were found in the last 4 weeks:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/de91eddc-def0-4335-a185-c96a3b692576

It hits with sanity quota also.

[21891.352731] task:ll_ost00_000    state:D stack:0     pid:589527 ppid:2      flags:0x80004080
[21891.354296] Call Trace:
[21891.354822]  __schedule+0x2d1/0x870
[21891.355529]  ? __wake_up_common_lock+0x89/0xc0
[21891.356386]  schedule+0x55/0xf0
[21891.357033]  jbd2_log_wait_commit+0xac/0x120 [jbd2]
[21891.357988]  ? finish_wait+0x80/0x80
[21891.358710]  ldiskfs_sync_file+0x365/0x3b0 [ldiskfs]
[21891.360706]  osd_object_sync+0x1b8/0x240 [osd_ldiskfs]
[21891.623381]  tgt_sync+0x161/0x260 [ptlrpc]
[21891.625292]  ofd_sync_hdl+0x161/0x5f0 [ofd]
[21894.729443]  ? req_capsule_server_pack+0x1e0/0x2b0 [ptlrpc]
[21894.730701]  tgt_request_handle+0x401/0x1d40 [ptlrpc]
[21894.732026]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x2ca/0xd70 [ptlrpc]
[21894.733300]  ? lprocfs_counter_add+0x117/0x180 [obdclass]
[21894.783337]  ptlrpc_main+0xbc2/0x1490 [ptlrpc]
[21894.784302]  ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0x5b0/0x5b0 [ptlrpc]
[21894.785428]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[21894.786085]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[21894.786931]  ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5f30d209-1fe4-45eb-a289-5829f6266e07

Still seeing this failure on master - 6x in the past week, out of 176 test runs ~= 1/30 runs.

Individual test runs take between 800-2400s, so this should add a check for "SLOW=yes" or skip the test otherwise.

It looks like the OST threads are stuck on jbd2 transactions:

Lustre: ll_ost00_039: service thread pid 36380 was inactive for 42.811 seconds. The thread might be hung, or it might only be slow and will resume later. Dumping the stack trace for debugging purposes:
 task:ll_ost00_010    state:D stack:0     pid:36005 tgid:36005 ppid:2      flags:0x00004000
 task:ll_ost00_039    state:D stack:0     pid:36380 tgid:36380 ppid:2      flags:0x00004000
 task:ll_ost00_008    state:D stack:0     pid:36003 tgid:36003 ppid:2      flags:0x00004000
 Call Trace:
  __schedule+0x229/0x550
  schedule+0x2e/0xd0
  jbd2_log_wait_commit+0xa3/0x110 [jbd2]
  ldiskfs_sync_file+0x1f7/0x390 [ldiskfs]
  osd_object_sync+0xd6/0x250 [osd_ldiskfs]
  tgt_sync+0x16d/0x270 [ptlrpc]
  ofd_sync_hdl+0xf6/0x470 [ofd]
  tgt_handle_request0+0x14a/0x770 [ptlrpc]
  tgt_request_handle+0x1eb/0xb80 [ptlrpc]
  ptlrpc_server_handle_request.isra.0+0x2e8/0xd80 [ptlrpc]
  ptlrpc_main+0xa7b/0xfa0 [ptlrpc]
  kthread+0xe0/0x100

The jbd2 thread is stuck on IO?

 task:jbd2/dm-13-8    state:D stack:0     pid:22716 tgid:22716 ppid:2      flags:0x00004000
 Call Trace:
  __schedule+0x229/0x550
  schedule+0x2e/0xd0
  io_schedule+0x42/0x70
  bit_wait_io+0xd/0x60
  __wait_on_bit+0x4b/0x150
  out_of_line_wait_on_bit+0x92/0xb0
  jbd2_journal_commit_transaction+0x14c3/0x18c0 [jbd2]
  kjournald2+0xa9/0x260 [jbd2]
  kthread+0xe0/0x100
being fixed under LU-17224, where this failure was introduced.
